1	import { z } from "zod"
2	
3	// Note: if you edit the schema here, you must also edit the schema in the
4	// apps/v4/public/schema/registry-item.json file.
5	
6	export const registryConfigItemSchema = z.union([
7	  // Simple string format: "https://example.com/{name}.json"
8	  z.string().refine((s) => s.includes("{name}"), {
9	    message: "Registry URL must include {name} placeholder",
10	  }),
11	  // Advanced object format with auth options
12	  z.object({
13	    url: z.string().refine((s) => s.includes("{name}"), {
14	      message: "Registry URL must include {name} placeholder",
15	    }),
16	    params: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
17	    headers: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
18	  }),
19	])
20	
21	export const registryConfigSchema = z.record(
22	  z.string().refine((key) => key.startsWith("@"), {
23	    message: "Registry names must start with @ (e.g., @v0, @acme)",
24	  }),
25	  registryConfigItemSchema
26	)
27	
28	export const rawConfigSchema = z
29	  .object({
30	    $schema: z.string().optional(),
31	    style: z.string(),
32	    rsc: z.coerce.boolean().default(false),
33	    tsx: z.coerce.boolean().default(true),
34	    tailwind: z.object({
35	      config: z.string().optional(),
36	      css: z.string(),
37	      baseColor: z.string(),
38	      cssVariables: z.boolean().default(true),
39	      prefix: z.string().default("").optional(),
40	    }),
41	    iconLibrary: z.string().optional(),
42	    rtl: z.coerce.boolean().default(false).optional(),
43	    menuColor: z
44	      .enum([
45	        "default",
46	        "inverted",
47	        "default-translucent",
48	        "inverted-translucent",
49	      ])
50	      .default("default")
51	      .optional(),
52	    menuAccent: z.enum(["subtle", "bold"]).default("subtle").optional(),
53	    aliases: z.object({
54	      components: z.string(),
55	      utils: z.string(),
56	      ui: z.string().optional(),
57	      lib: z.string().optional(),
58	      hooks: z.string().optional(),
59	    }),
60	    registries: registryConfigSchema.optional(),
61	  })
62	  .strict()
63	
64	export const configSchema = rawConfigSchema.extend({
65	  resolvedPaths: z.object({
66	    cwd: z.string(),
67	    tailwindConfig: z.string(),
68	    tailwindCss: z.string(),
69	    utils: z.string(),
70	    components: z.string(),
71	    lib: z.string(),
72	    hooks: z.string(),
73	    ui: z.string(),
74	  }),
75	})
76	
77	// TODO: type the key.
78	// Okay for now since I don't want a breaking change.
79	export const workspaceConfigSchema = z.record(configSchema)
80	
81	export const registryItemTypeSchema = z.enum([
82	  "registry:lib",
83	  "registry:block",
84	  "registry:component",
85	  "registry:ui",
86	  "registry:hook",
87	  "registry:page",
88	  "registry:file",
89	  "registry:theme",
90	  "registry:style",
91	  "registry:item",
92	  "registry:base",
93	  "registry:font",
94	
95	  // Internal use only.
96	  "registry:example",
97	  "registry:internal",
98	])
99	
100	export const registryItemFileSchema = z.discriminatedUnion("type", [
101	  // Target is required for registry:file and registry:page
102	  z.object({
103	    path: z.string(),
104	    content: z.string().optional(),
105	    type: z.enum(["registry:file", "registry:page"]),
106	    target: z.string(),
107	  }),
108	  z.object({
109	    path: z.string(),
110	    content: z.string().optional(),
111	    type: registryItemTypeSchema.exclude(["registry:file", "registry:page"]),
112	    target: z.string().optional(),
113	  }),
114	])
115	
116	export const registryItemTailwindSchema = z.object({
117	  config: z
118	    .object({
119	      content: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
120	      theme: z.record(z.string(), z.any()).optional(),
121	      plugins: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
122	    })
123	    .optional(),
124	})
125	
126	export const registryItemCssVarsSchema = z.object({
127	  theme: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
128	  light: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
129	  dark: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
130	})
131	
132	// Recursive type for CSS properties that supports empty objects at any level.
133	const cssValueSchema: z.ZodType<any> = z.lazy(() =>
134	  z.union([
135	    z.string(),
136	    z.array(z.union([z.string(), z.record(z.string(), z.string())])),
137	    z.record(z.string(), cssValueSchema),
138	  ])
139	)
140	
141	export const registryItemCssSchema = z.record(z.string(), cssValueSchema)
142	
143	export const registryItemEnvVarsSchema = z.record(z.string(), z.string())
144	
145	// Font metadata schema for registry:font items.
146	export const registryItemFontSchema = z.object({
147	  family: z.string(),
148	  provider: z.literal("google"),
149	  import: z.string(),
150	  variable: z.string(),
151	  weight: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
152	  subsets: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
153	  selector: z.string().optional(),
154	  dependency: z.string().optional(),
155	})
156	
157	// Common fields shared by all registry items.
158	export const registryItemCommonSchema = z.object({
159	  $schema: z.string().optional(),
160	  extends: z.string().optional(),
161	  name: z.string(),
162	  title: z.string().optional(),
163	  author: z.string().min(2).optional(),
164	  description: z.string().optional(),
165	  dependencies: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
166	  devDependencies: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
167	  registryDependencies: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
168	  files: z.array(registryItemFileSchema).optional(),
169	  tailwind: registryItemTailwindSchema.optional(),
170	  cssVars: registryItemCssVarsSchema.optional(),
171	  css: registryItemCssSchema.optional(),
172	  envVars: registryItemEnvVarsSchema.optional(),
173	  meta: z.record(z.string(), z.any()).optional(),
174	  docs: z.string().optional(),
175	  categories: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
176	})
177	
178	// registry:base has a config field, registry:font has a font field.
179	export const registryItemSchema = z.discriminatedUnion("type", [
180	  registryItemCommonSchema.extend({
181	    type: z.literal("registry:base"),
182	    config: rawConfigSchema.deepPartial().optional(),
183	  }),
184	  registryItemCommonSchema.extend({
185	    type: z.literal("registry:font"),
186	    font: registryItemFontSchema,
187	  }),
188	  registryItemCommonSchema.extend({
189	    type: registryItemTypeSchema.exclude(["registry:base", "registry:font"]),
190	  }),
191	])
192	
193	export type RegistryItem = z.infer<typeof registryItemSchema>
194	
195	// Helper type for registry:base items specifically.
196	export type RegistryBaseItem = Extract<RegistryItem, { type: "registry:base" }>
197	
198	// Helper type for registry:font items specifically.
199	export type RegistryFontItem = Extract<RegistryItem, { type: "registry:font" }>
200	
201	export const registrySchema = z.object({
202	  name: z.string(),
203	  homepage: z.string(),
204	  items: z.array(registryItemSchema),
205	})
206	
207	export type Registry = z.infer<typeof registrySchema>
208	
209	export const registryIndexSchema = z.array(registryItemSchema)
210	
211	export const stylesSchema = z.array(
212	  z.object({
213	    name: z.string(),
214	    label: z.string(),
215	  })
216	)
217	
218	export const iconsSchema = z.record(
219	  z.string(),
220	  z.record(z.string(), z.string())
221	)
222	
223	export const registryBaseColorSchema = z.object({
224	  inlineColors: z.object({
225	    light: z.record(z.string(), z.string()),
226	    dark: z.record(z.string(), z.string()),
227	  }),
228	  cssVars: registryItemCssVarsSchema,
229	  cssVarsV4: registryItemCssVarsSchema.optional(),
230	  inlineColorsTemplate: z.string(),
231	  cssVarsTemplate: z.string(),
232	})
233	
234	export const registryResolvedItemsTreeSchema = registryItemCommonSchema
235	  .pick({
236	    dependencies: true,
237	    devDependencies: true,
238	    files: true,
239	    tailwind: true,
240	    cssVars: true,
241	    css: true,
242	    envVars: true,
243	    docs: true,
244	  })
245	  .extend({
246	    fonts: z
247	      .array(
248	        registryItemCommonSchema.extend({
249	          type: z.literal("registry:font"),
250	          font: registryItemFontSchema,
251	        })
252	      )
253	      .optional(),
254	  })
255	
256	export const searchResultItemSchema = z.object({
257	  name: z.string(),
258	  type: z.string().optional(),
259	  description: z.string().optional(),
260	  registry: z.string(),
261	  addCommandArgument: z.string(),
262	})
263	
264	export const searchResultsSchema = z.object({
265	  pagination: z.object({
266	    total: z.number(),
267	    offset: z.number(),
268	    limit: z.number(),
269	    hasMore: z.boolean(),
270	  }),
271	  items: z.array(searchResultItemSchema),
272	})
273	
274	// Legacy schema for getRegistriesIndex() backward compatibility.
275	export const registriesIndexSchema = z.record(

278	)
279	
280	// New schema for getRegistries().
281	export const registriesSchema = z.array(
282	  z.object({
283	    name: z.string(),
284	    homepage: z.string().optional(),
285	    url: z.string(),
286	    description: z.string().optional(),
287	  })
288	)
289	
290	export const presetSchema = z.object({
291	  name: z.string(),
292	  title: z.string(),
293	  description: z.string(),
294	  base: z.string(),
295	  style: z.string(),
296	  baseColor: z.string(),
297	  theme: z.string(),
298	  iconLibrary: z.string(),
299	  font: z.string(),
300	  rtl: z.coerce.boolean().default(false),
301	  menuAccent: z.enum(["subtle", "bold"]),
302	  menuColor: z.enum([
303	    "default",
304	    "inverted",
305	    "default-translucent",
306	    "inverted-translucent",

308	  radius: z.string(),
309	})
310	
311	export type Preset = z.infer<typeof presetSchema>
312

27	)

29	function Toggle({
30	  className,

36	export type FontValue = (typeof fontValues)[number]

39	export function getBodyFont(font: FontValue) {
40	  return bodyFonts.find((item) => item.name === `font-${font}`)
41	}
42	
43	export function getHeadingFont(
44	  fontHeading: Exclude<FontHeadingValue, "inherit">
45	) {
46	  return headingFonts.find(
47	    (item) => item.name === `font-heading-${fontHeading}`
48	  )
49	}

51	export function getInheritedHeadingFontValue(font: FontValue) {

2	  "name": "shadcn",

64	    "dev": "tsup --watch",
65	    "build": "tsup",
66	    "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit",
67	    "clean": "rimraf dist && rimraf components",
68	    "start:dev": "cross-env REGISTRY_URL=http://localhost:4000/r SHADCN_TEMPLATE_DIR=../../templates node dist/index.js",
69	    "start:prod": "cross-env REGISTRY_URL=https://ui.shadcn.com/r node dist/index.js",
70	    "start": "node dist/index.js",
71	    "format:write": "prettier --write \"**/*.{ts,tsx,mdx}\" --cache",
72	    "format:check": "prettier --check \"**/*.{ts,tsx,mdx}\" --cache",
73	    "release": "changeset version",
74	    "pub:beta": "pnpm build && pnpm publish --no-git-checks --access public --tag beta",
75	    "pub:next": "pnpm build && pnpm publish --no-git-checks --access public --tag next",
76	    "pub:release": "pnpm build && pnpm publish --access public",
77	    "test": "vitest run",
78	    "test:dev": "REGISTRY_URL=http://localhost:4000/r SHADCN_TEMPLATE_DIR=../../templates vitest run",
79	    "mcp:inspect": "pnpm dlx @modelcontextprotocol/inspector node dist/index.js mcp"

Excellent! I have all the information I need. Let me compile the comprehensive report now. ## Comprehensive Report: shadcn/ui Demo and Component Structure I've thoroughly explored the shadcn/ui codebase. Here's what you need to know for adding a Stepper component: --- ### 1. DEMO FILES LOCATION & NAMING CONVENTION **Primary locations:** - `/apps/v4/examples/{base}/` - Base examples (accordion-demo.tsx, tabs-demo.tsx, etc.) - `/apps/v4/registry/{style}/examples/` - Style-specific examples (e.g., new-york-v4/examples/) **Naming convention:** `{component-name}-demo.tsx` (or variant names like `accordion-content.tsx`, `tabs-content.tsx`) **Directory structure:** ``` apps/v4/ examples/ base/ ├── accordion-demo.tsx ├── tabs-demo.tsx ├── breadcrumb-demo.tsx └── [60+ other examples] registry/ new-york-v4/ examples/ ├── _registry.ts (registry config) ├── accordion-demo.tsx ├── tabs-demo.tsx └── [100+ examples per style] ui/ ├── accordion.tsx ├── tabs.tsx ├── breadcrumb.tsx └── [57 total UI components] ``` --- ### 2. COMPLETE DEMO EXAMPLE FILES #### Accordion Demo **Path:** `/apps/v4/examples/base/accordion-demo.tsx` ```tsx import { Accordion, AccordionContent, AccordionItem, AccordionTrigger, } from "@/styles/base-nova/ui/accordion" export default function AccordionDemo() { return ( What are your shipping options? We offer standard (5-7 days), express (2-3 days), and overnight shipping. Free shipping on international orders. What is your return policy? Returns accepted within 30 days. Items must be unused and in original packaging. Refunds processed within 5-7 business days. How can I contact customer support? Reach us via email, live chat, or phone. We respond within 24 hours during business days. ) } ``` #### Tabs Demo **Path:** `/apps/v4/examples/base/tabs-demo.tsx` ```tsx import { Card, CardContent, CardDescription, CardHeader, CardTitle, } from "@/styles/base-nova/ui/card" import { Tabs, TabsContent, TabsList, TabsTrigger, } from "@/styles/base-nova/ui/tabs" export function TabsDemo() { return ( Overview Analytics Reports Settings Overview View your key metrics and recent project activity. Track progress across all your active projects. You have 12 active projects and 3 pending tasks. Analytics Track performance and user engagement metrics. Monitor trends and identify growth opportunities. Page views are up 25% compared to last month. Reports Generate and download your detailed reports. Export data in multiple formats for analysis. You have 5 reports ready and available to export. Settings Manage your account preferences and options. Customize your experience to fit your needs. Configure notifications, security, and themes. ) } ``` #### Breadcrumb Demo (new-york-v4 style) **Path:** `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/breadcrumb-demo.tsx` ```tsx import Link from "next/link" import { Breadcrumb, BreadcrumbEllipsis, BreadcrumbItem, BreadcrumbLink, BreadcrumbList, BreadcrumbPage, BreadcrumbSeparator, } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/breadcrumb" import { DropdownMenu, DropdownMenuContent, DropdownMenuItem, DropdownMenuTrigger, } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/dropdown-menu" export default function BreadcrumbDemo() { return ( Home Toggle menu Documentation Themes GitHub Components Breadcrumb ) } ``` --- ### 3. BLOCKS/PAGE TEMPLATES **Location:** `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/blocks/` **Structure:** Blocks are composite pages that showcase multiple components together. Examples include: - `dashboard-01/` - Full dashboard with sidebar, charts, and data table - `login-01/` through `login-05/` - Login form variations - `sidebar-01/` through `sidebar-13/` - Sidebar navigation templates **Block structure:** ``` blocks/dashboard-01/ ├── page.tsx (main page component) ├── data.json (sample data) └── components/ ├── app-sidebar.tsx ├── chart-area-interactive.tsx ├── data-table.tsx ├── nav-main.tsx ├── section-cards.tsx └── ... ``` **Example block page:** ```tsx // /apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/blocks/dashboard-01/page.tsx export default function Page() { return (
) } ``` --- ### 4. COMPONENT IMPORT PATTERNS **Two main patterns used:** **Pattern A: Base styles (older structure)** ```tsx import { Accordion, AccordionContent } from "@/styles/base-nova/ui/accordion" ``` **Pattern B: Registry-based (newer structure - preferred)** ```tsx import { Tabs, TabsContent } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/tabs" ``` **Key observation:** The newer `registry` pattern is the standard. Import path follows: ``` @/registry/{style-name}/ui/{component-name} ``` --- ### 5. HOW COMPONENTS ARE SURFACED IN DOCS **Documentation files location:** - `/apps/v4/content/docs/components/base/` (accordion.mdx, tabs.mdx, etc.) - `/apps/v4/content/docs/components/radix/` (Radix-based variants) **Component index page:** - `/apps/v4/content/docs/components/index.mdx` - Uses `` component to auto-render all components **Example MDX frontmatter:** ```mdx --- title: Accordion description: A vertically stacked set of interactive headings that each reveal a section of content. base: base component: true links: doc: https://base-ui.com/react/components/accordion api: https://base-ui.com/react/components/accordion#api-reference --- ## Installation ``` **Registration system:** Uses MDX frontmatter with `component: true` to auto-discover and list components. --- ### 6. ## COMPREHENSIVE SHADCN/UI STEPPER COMPONENT ADDITION GUIDE

Based on thorough exploration of the shadcn/ui codebase, here's everything you need to know to add a Stepper compound component:

### 1. TOP-LEVEL DIRECTORY STRUCTURE

**Root Directory**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775473741916-2gb8cb/`

Key directories and files:
- **`/packages/shadcn/`** - The main shadcn CLI package (published to npm)
  - `/src/registry/` - Registry system implementation (schemas, resolvers, validators)
  - `/src/schema/` - Zod schemas defining the registry item format
  - `/src/tailwind.css` - Base animations and custom Tailwind utilities
  
- **`/apps/v4/`** - The Next.js demo/documentation app (the UI playground)
  - `/registry/new-york-v4/` - The "new-york" style registry
    - `/ui/` - Component implementations
    - `/examples/` - Demo components
    - `/ui/_registry.ts` - Registry entries for UI components
    - `/examples/_registry.ts` - Registry entries for example/demo components
  - `components.json` - Project's shadcn configuration
  - `registry.json` - Generated registry manifest (output of build)

- **`pnpm-workspace.yaml`** - Monorepo configuration (pnpm workspaces)
- **`tailwindcss`, `radix-ui`, `lucide-react`** - Core dependencies

### 2. REGISTRY SYSTEM - HOW IT WORKS

#### Registry Files Location
- **TypeScript Registry Definitions**: `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts` (UI components)
- **Generated Registry JSON**: `/apps/v4/registry.json` (built output, consumed by CLI)
- **Schema Definition**: `/packages/shadcn/src/registry/schema.ts`

#### Registry Item Type
From the Zod schema (`schema.ts`), these are the valid registry types:
```
"registry:lib" | "registry:block" | "registry:component" | "registry:ui" | 
"registry:hook" | "registry:page" | "registry:file" | "registry:theme" | 
"registry:style" | "registry:item" | "registry:base" | "registry:font" |
"registry:example" | "registry:internal"
```

#### Registry Item Structure (TypeScript Format)
Each item in the `_registry.ts` file follows this schema:

```typescript
{
  name: string,                              // Unique identifier (kebab-case)
  type: "registry:ui" | "registry:example",  // Item type
  dependencies?: string[],                   // npm packages (e.g., ["radix-ui"])
  devDependencies?: string[],                // dev-only packages
  registryDependencies?: string[],           // Other registry components this depends on
  files: Array<{
    path: string,                            // Path to component file (relative to style dir)
    type: "registry:ui" | "registry:example"
  }>,
  tailwind?: {                               // Optional: Tailwind config to merge
    config?: {
      content?: string[],
      theme?: Record<string, any>,
      plugins?: string[]
    }
  },
  cssVars?: {                                // Optional: CSS variables
    light?: Record<string, string>,
    dark?: Record<string, string>,
    theme?: Record<string, string>
  },
  css?: Record<string, any>,                 // Optional: Custom CSS
  docs?: string,                             // Optional: Setup instructions (markdown)
  meta?: Record<string, any>,                // Optional: Custom metadata
  categories?: string[]                      // Optional: Category tags
}
```

### 3. EXISTING COMPOUND COMPONENTS - STRUCTURE PATTERNS

#### ACCORDION (Multi-part, Radix-based)
**File**: `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/accordion.tsx`

**Components Exported**:
- `Accordion` (Root wrapper)
- `AccordionItem` (Container for trigger + content)
- `AccordionTrigger` (Clickable header)
- `AccordionContent` (Collapsible content area)

**Registry Entry** (from `_registry.ts`):
```typescript
{
  name: "accordion",
  type: "registry:ui",
  dependencies: ["radix-ui"],  // Installs @radix-ui/react-accordion
  files: [{
    path: "ui/accordion.tsx",
    type: "registry:ui"
  }]
}
```

**Key Patterns**:
- Wraps Radix-UI primitives (e.g., `AccordionPrimitive.Root`, `AccordionPrimitive.Item`)
- Uses `data-slot="accordion*"` attributes for styling hooks
- Uses `cn()` utility from `@/lib/utils` for class merging
- Exports all sub-components for composition
- Uses `"use client"` directive (client-side component)

#### TABS (Multi-part with variants)
**File**: `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/tabs.tsx`

**Components Exported**:
- `Tabs` (Root)
- `TabsList` (Container for triggers, with variant support)
- `TabsTrigger` (Individual tab button)
- `TabsContent` (Content panel)
- `tabsListVariants` (CVA export for styling)

**Pattern**: Radix-UI based, uses `class-variance-authority` (CVA) for complex styling variants

#### BREADCRUMB (HTML-semantic)
**File**: `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/breadcrumb.tsx`

**Components Exported**:
- `Breadcrumb` (nav element)
- `BreadcrumbList` (ol element)
- `BreadcrumbItem` (li element)
- `BreadcrumbLink` (a element, supports `asChild` for polymorphism)
- `BreadcrumbPage` (span for current page)
- `BreadcrumbSeparator` (li with visual separator)
- `BreadcrumbEllipsis` (li for collapsed items)

**Key Patterns**:
- Uses semantic HTML (nav, ol, li, a, span)
- Uses Radix's `Slot` component for `asChild` pattern (component as child polymorphism)
- No Radix primitive, fully custom styled
- Each sub-component is a simple styled wrapper

#### SIDEBAR (Complex, multi-dependency)
**File**: `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/sidebar.tsx`

**Registry Entry**:
```typescript
{
  name: "sidebar",
  type: "registry:ui",
  dependencies: ["radix-ui", "class-variance-authority", "lucide-react"],
  registryDependencies: ["button", "separator", "sheet", "tooltip", "input", "use-mobile", "skeleton"],
  files: [{
    path: "ui/sidebar.tsx",
    type: "registry:ui"
  }],
  tailwind: {
    config: {
      theme: {
        extend: {
          colors: {
            sidebar: { /* custom color palette */ }
          }
        }
      }
    }
  },
  cssVars: {
    light: { "sidebar-background": "0 0% 98%", ... },
    dark: { "sidebar-background": "240 5.9% 10%", ... }
  }
}
```

**Key Patterns**:
- Multiple npm dependencies
- `registryDependencies` (other shadcn components it needs)
- Custom Tailwind theme extension
- CSS variables for theming (light/dark support)

#### ALERT-DIALOG (Radix-based with sub-components)
**File**: `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/alert-dialog.tsx`

**Components Exported**:
- `AlertDialog` (Root)
- `AlertDialogTrigger`
- `AlertDialogPortal`
- `AlertDialogOverlay`
- `AlertDialogContent`
- `AlertDialogHeader`, `AlertDialogFooter` (layout components)
- `AlertDialogTitle`, `AlertDialogDescription`
- `AlertDialogMedia`
- `AlertDialogAction`, `AlertDialogCancel` (Button variants wrapping Radix actions)

**Uses Button Component**: Imports and uses the Button component inside AlertDialogAction/Cancel

### 4. DEMO/EXAMPLE FILES - FORMAT & REGISTRATION

**Location**: `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/`

**Naming Convention**: `{component-name}-{variant}.tsx`
Examples: `accordion-demo.tsx`, `breadcrumb-demo.tsx`, `breadcrumb-responsive.tsx`

**Example File Format**:
```typescript
// Direct import from registry path
import { Accordion, AccordionContent, AccordionItem, AccordionTrigger } 
  from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/accordion"

export default function AccordionDemo() {
  return (
    // Component usage demo
  )
}
```

**Registration in _registry.ts** (`/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts`):
```typescript
{
  name: "accordion-demo",        // Unique name
  type: "registry:example",      // Type is always "registry:example"
  registryDependencies: ["accordion"],  // Which UI components it imports
  files: [{

### 5. KEY PATTERNS & CONVENTIONS

Components use `data-slot="component-name"` for styling hooks:
```typescript
<AccordionPrimitive.Root data-slot="accordion" {...props} />
<AccordionPrimitive.Item data-slot="accordion-item" {...props} />
<AccordionPrimitive.Trigger data-slot="accordion-trigger" {...props} />
```

#### Utility Import
All components import from `@/lib/utils`:
```typescript

```
The `cn()` function merges Tailwind classes (uses `tailwind-merge` + `clsx`)

#### Client Directive
Components using React hooks or interactivity need `"use client"`:
```typescript

#### Component Composition
Sub-components are exported individually for composition:

When using Radix-UI:

}
```

#### CVA (Class Variance Authority) for Variants
```typescript
import { cva } from "class-variance-authority"

const tabsListVariants = cva("base-classes", {
  variants: {
    variant: {
      default: "default-classes",
      line: "line-classes"
    },
    defaultVariants: {
      variant: "default"
    }
  }
})

function TabsList({ variant = "default", ...props }: ... & VariantProps<typeof tabsListVariants>) {
  return <div className={cn(tabsListVariants({ variant }), className)} />
}
```

## COMPREHENSIVE SHADCN/UI STEPPER COMPONENT ADDITION GUIDE

Based on thorough exploration of the shadcn/ui codebase, here's everything you need to know to add a Stepper compound component:

### 1. TOP-LEVEL DIRECTORY STRUCTURE

**Root Directory**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775473741916-2gb8cb/`

Key directories and files:
- **`/packages/shadcn/`** - The main shadcn CLI package (published to npm)
  - `/src/registry/` - Registry system implementation (schemas, resolvers, validators)
  - `/src/schema/` - Zod schemas defining the registry item format
  - `/src/tailwind.css` - Base animations and custom Tailwind utilities
  
- **`/apps/v4/`** - The Next.js demo/documentation app (the UI playground)
  - `/registry/new-york-v4/` - The "new-york" style registry
    - `/ui/` - Component implementations
    - `/examples/` - Demo components
    - `/ui/_registry.ts` - Registry entries for UI components
    - `/examples/_registry.ts` - Registry entries for example/demo components
  - `components.json` - Project's shadcn configuration
  - `registry.json` - Generated registry manifest (output of build)

- **`pnpm-workspace.yaml`** - Monorepo configuration (pnpm workspaces)
- **`tailwindcss`, `radix-ui`, `lucide-react`** - Core dependencies

### 2. REGISTRY SYSTEM - HOW IT WORKS

#### Registry Files Location
- **TypeScript Registry Definitions**: `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts` (UI components)
- **Generated Registry JSON**: `/apps/v4/registry.json` (built output, consumed by CLI)
- **Schema Definition**: `/packages/shadcn/src/registry/schema.ts`

#### Registry Item Type
From the Zod schema (`schema.ts`), these are the valid registry types:
```
"registry:lib" | "registry:block" | "registry:component" | "registry:ui" | 
"registry:hook" | "registry:page" | "registry:file" | "registry:theme" | 
"registry:style" | "registry:item" | "registry:base" | "registry:font" |
"registry:example" | "registry:internal"
```

#### Registry Item Structure (TypeScript Format)
Each item in the `_registry.ts` file follows this schema:

```typescript
{
  name: string,                              // Unique identifier (kebab-case)
  type: "registry:ui" | "registry:example",  // Item type
  dependencies?: string[],                   // npm packages (e.g., ["radix-ui"])
  devDependencies?: string[],                // dev-only packages
  registryDependencies?: string[],           // Other registry components this depends on
  files: Array<{
    path: string,                            // Path to component file (relative to style dir)
    type: "registry:ui" | "registry:example"
  }>,
  tailwind?: {                               // Optional: Tailwind config to merge
    config?: {
      content?: string[],
      theme?: Record<string, any>,
      plugins?: string[]
    }
  },
  cssVars?: {                                // Optional: CSS variables
    light?: Record<string, string>,
    dark?: Record<string, string>,
    theme?: Record<string, string>
  },
  css?: Record<string, any>,                 // Optional: Custom CSS
  docs?: string,                             // Optional: Setup instructions (markdown)
  meta?: Record<string, any>,                // Optional: Custom metadata
  categories?: string[]                      // Optional: Category tags
}
```

### 3. EXISTING COMPOUND COMPONENTS - STRUCTURE PATTERNS

#### ACCORDION (Multi-part, Radix-based)
**File**: `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/accordion.tsx`

**Components Exported**:
- `Accordion` (Root wrapper)
- `AccordionItem` (Container for trigger + content)
- `AccordionTrigger` (Clickable header)
- `AccordionContent` (Collapsible content area)

**Registry Entry** (from `_registry.ts`):
```typescript
{
  name: "accordion",
  type: "registry:ui",
  dependencies: ["radix-ui"],  // Installs @radix-ui/react-accordion
  files: [{
    path: "ui/accordion.tsx",
    type: "registry:ui"
  }]
}
```

**Key Patterns**:
- Wraps Radix-UI primitives (e.g., `AccordionPrimitive.Root`, `AccordionPrimitive.Item`)
- Uses `data-slot="accordion*"` attributes for styling hooks
- Uses `cn()` utility from `@/lib/utils` for class merging
- Exports all sub-components for composition
- Uses `"use client"` directive (client-side component)

#### TABS (Multi-part with variants)
**File**: `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/tabs.tsx`

**Components Exported**:
- `Tabs` (Root)
- `TabsList` (Container for triggers, with variant support)
- `TabsTrigger` (Individual tab button)
- `TabsContent` (Content panel)
- `tabsListVariants` (CVA export for styling)

**Pattern**: Radix-UI based, uses `class-variance-authority` (CVA) for complex styling variants

#### BREADCRUMB (HTML-semantic)
**File**: `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/breadcrumb.tsx`

**Components Exported**:
- `Breadcrumb` (nav element)
- `BreadcrumbList` (ol element)
- `BreadcrumbItem` (li element)
- `BreadcrumbLink` (a element, supports `asChild` for polymorphism)
- `BreadcrumbPage` (span for current page)
- `BreadcrumbSeparator` (li with visual separator)
- `BreadcrumbEllipsis` (li for collapsed items)

**Key Patterns**:
- Uses semantic HTML (nav, ol, li, a, span)
- Uses Radix's `Slot` component for `asChild` pattern (component as child polymorphism)
- No Radix primitive, fully custom styled
- Each sub-component is a simple styled wrapper

#### SIDEBAR (Complex, multi-dependency)
**File**: `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/sidebar.tsx`

**Registry Entry**:
```typescript
{
  name: "sidebar",
  type: "registry:ui",
  dependencies: ["radix-ui", "class-variance-authority", "lucide-react"],
  registryDependencies: ["button", "separator", "sheet", "tooltip", "input", "use-mobile", "skeleton"],
  files: [{
    path: "ui/sidebar.tsx",
    type: "registry:ui"
  }],
  tailwind: {
    config: {
      theme: {
        extend: {
          colors: {
            sidebar: { /* custom color palette */ }
          }
        }
      }
    }
  },
  cssVars: {
    light: { "sidebar-background": "0 0% 98%", ... },
    dark: { "sidebar-background": "240 5.9% 10%", ... }
  }
}
```

**Key Patterns**:
- Multiple npm dependencies
- `registryDependencies` (other shadcn components it needs)
- Custom Tailwind theme extension
- CSS variables for theming (light/dark support)

#### ALERT-DIALOG (Radix-based with sub-components)
**File**: `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/alert-dialog.tsx`

**Components Exported**:
- `AlertDialog` (Root)
- `AlertDialogTrigger`
- `AlertDialogPortal`
- `AlertDialogOverlay`

- `AlertDialogHeader`, `AlertDialogFooter` (layout components)
- `AlertDialogTitle`, `AlertDialogDescription`
- `AlertDialogMedia`
- `AlertDialogAction`, `AlertDialogCancel` (Button variants wrapping Radix actions)

**Uses Button Component**: Imports and uses the Button component inside AlertDialogAction/Cancel

1	"use client"
2	
3	import * as React from "react"
4	import { type VariantProps } from "class-variance-authority"
5	import { ToggleGroup as ToggleGroupPrimitive } from "radix-ui"
6	
7	import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"
8	import { toggleVariants } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/toggle"
9	
10	const ToggleGroupContext = React.createContext<
11	  VariantProps<typeof toggleVariants> & {
12	    spacing?: number
13	  }
14	>({
15	  size: "default",
16	  variant: "default",
17	  spacing: 0,
18	})
19	
20	function ToggleGroup({
21	  className,
22	  variant,
23	  size,
24	  spacing = 0,
25	  children,
26	  ...props
27	}: React.ComponentProps<typeof ToggleGroupPrimitive.Root> &
28	  VariantProps<typeof toggleVariants> & {
29	    spacing?: number
30	  }) {
31	  return (
32	    <ToggleGroupPrimitive.Root
33	      data-slot="toggle-group"
34	      data-variant={variant}
35	      data-size={size}
36	      data-spacing={spacing}
37	      style={{ "--gap": spacing } as React.CSSProperties}
38	      className={cn(
39	        "group/toggle-group flex w-fit items-center gap-[--spacing(var(--gap))] rounded-md data-[spacing=default]:data-[variant=outline]:shadow-xs",
40	        className
41	      )}
42	      {...props}
43	    >
44	      <ToggleGroupContext.Provider value={{ variant, size, spacing }}>
45	        {children}
46	      </ToggleGroupContext.Provider>
47	    </ToggleGroupPrimitive.Root>
48	  )
49	}
50	
51	function ToggleGroupItem({
52	  className,
53	  children,
54	  variant,
55	  size,
56	  ...props
57	}: React.ComponentProps<typeof ToggleGroupPrimitive.Item> &
58	  VariantProps<typeof toggleVariants>) {
59	  const context = React.useContext(ToggleGroupContext)
60	
61	  return (
62	    <ToggleGroupPrimitive.Item
63	      data-slot="toggle-group-item"
64	      data-variant={context.variant || variant}
65	      data-size={context.size || size}
66	      data-spacing={context.spacing}
67	      className={cn(
68	        toggleVariants({
69	          variant: context.variant || variant,
70	          size: context.size || size,
71	        }),
72	        "w-auto min-w-0 shrink-0 px-3 focus:z-10 focus-visible:z-10",
73	        "data-[spacing=0]:rounded-none data-[spacing=0]:shadow-none data-[spacing=0]:first:rounded-l-md data-[spacing=0]:last:rounded-r-md data-[spacing=0]:data-[variant=outline]:border-l-0 data-[spacing=0]:data-[variant=outline]:first:border-l",
74	        className
75	      )}
76	      {...props}
77	    >
78	      {children}
79	    </ToggleGroupPrimitive.Item>
80	  )
81	}
82	
83	export { ToggleGroup, ToggleGroupItem }
84

Let me now design the implementation approach and write the plan.


I'm mapping out the component architecture: the root Stepper component will manage state and provide context to child components, StepperItem will represent individual steps with their own value and disabled state, and StepperTrigger will be a button element that displays the step indicator and handles navigation.

The remaining components—StepperTitle, StepperDescription, StepperSeparator, and StepperContent—handle the visual presentation and content areas for each step.

For context, I'm defining two layers: a root context tracking the active step value and orientation, plus an item-level context for each step's specific state. The Stepper itself needs to support both controlled mode with value and onValueChange props, and uncontrolled mode for simpler use cases.

For determining step status, I need to compare each step's value against the current active value to know if it's active, and I'll need to track completion based on step indices—so the root Stepper component should maintain awareness of all step values to calculate whether a step is completed or still pending. I think the cleanest approach is to automatically mark all steps before the current one as completed—that works well for a wizard-style stepper and keeps the API simple for users. component. Let's use:
- `Stepper` takes `value` (active step string)
- `Stepper` takes `steps?: string[]` (ordered list of step IDs) to determine completion
- If `steps` not provided, `StepperItem` just uses data-state="active" when matching

Actually, let me reconsider the API design. The most intuitive pattern would have `Stepper` wrap `StepperItem` components with `StepperSeparator` between them, where each item has its own `value` prop. The real question is how `StepperItem` determines if it's completed—I could either have items register themselves with the parent context on mount, pass an explicit `completedSteps` array to `Stepper`, or let users manually control completion with a `completed` prop on each item. The registration approach feels cleanest for a wizard flow since items would naturally know their completion state based on render order. I'm realizing the registration approach via useEffect could have ordering issues, so I'm reconsidering simpler alternatives—either exposing a `completed` prop directly on StepperItem or creating a LinearStepper wrapper that manages steps internally. Looking at how other shadcn extensions handle this, I think the most pragmatic approach is to let the Stepper root hold the active value and derive completion status from the step order.

Actually, even useLayoutEffect registration has concerns in strict mode. This gives flexibility without forcing magic registration logic.

On second thought, the simplest approach is just an explicit `completed` prop on StepperItem—users decide what "completed" means, no registration needed. It's straightforward to implement, easy to understand, and works for non-linear steppers too.

For horizontal layouts, I could put the triggers in a row at the top and then render the content panels below, keeping them outside the item containers so they can span the full width.

Alternatively, I could nest the content directly within each item, which works better for vertical steppers but feels awkward when displaying horizontally since the content would need special styling to appear below the entire trigger row rather than inline. I'm realizing the complexity here—trying to keep StepperContent nested inside StepperItem while supporting both vertical and horizontal layouts is tricky, especially when horizontal needs the triggers in a separate row. Let me settle on a pragmatic approach: vertical works naturally with the nested structure, and for horizontal I'll accept that the layout shifts slightly but the component API stays consistent. both horizontal and vertical steppers use `flex-col` at the root level, but the key difference is how the `StepperItem` components arrange themselves internally.
